description: Add a sanctioned config-repair path that applies the corrective mutation and validates the result, rather than loading-and-validating the stored config before the repair, so an already-invalid config is recoverable. Scope: any invalid config regardless of version, distinct from the version-gap migration. The repair mechanism is a `force`/`repair` opt on amend-config that bypasses the pre-load validation and validates the result via the existing write path (block-api.ts:892); derived from the existing op surface, no DEC.
acceptance_criteria: An invalid config, including schema_version equal to current with an invalid body, is restored to validity through a sanctioned CLI op without a forbidden direct edit of config.json.
The repair validates the corrected result, not the pre-existing stored config; it does not throw on load before the mutation applies.
Both the version-gap subset and the no-version-delta invalidity are recoverable.

The repair is a `force`/`repair` opt on amend-config that skips the pre-load validation at context.ts:1251 and relies on the result-validation already present in writeTypedFile (block-api.ts:892-895); it is the minimal in-grain extension of the existing dryRun opt (context.ts:1229), not a new validation discipline (result-validation already exists). A separate recover op is a heavier alternative, not required.
notes: Repair mechanism is DERIVED, not deferred to a DECISION: a `force`/`repair` opt on amend-config that skips the pre-load validation (context.ts:1251 loadConfigForDir) and relies on the result-validation already present on the write path (block-api.ts:892-895). No DEC and no task_governed_by_decision edge. Derivation: result-validation already universal on config writes (block-api.ts:892); the only blocker is the pre-load validation (context.ts:1251); amend already extends by opt (context.ts:1229, dryRun), so the force/repair opt is the minimal in-grain extension. A separate recover op is a heavier alternative, not required — a minimal-surface preference, not a DEC-mandated exclusion.
